Case 1-1 — Functional Localization in the Nervous System

Twelve case-based, board-style questions built directly from Willard's Orientation to Medical Neuroanatomy lecture and Case Study 1-1. Each question is a clinical vignette that tests the reasoning chain — VOR / doll's head logic, UMN vs. LMN acid tests, spinal shock, the four-compartment localization framework, posturing, stroke classification, and the herniation cascade — not isolated fact recall. Every choice is explained, including why each distractor is wrong.

12 questions 5 choices each Case-based / Willard style Learn + Exam modes Per-distractor explanations Highlight / Strikeout tools
